verdict_reasoning: B4GALT1 is a type II Golgi-resident glycosyltransferase with an N-terminal signal-anchor TM domain that directs and retains it in the Golgi lumen-facing (inner) membrane. The N-terminal hydrophobic sequence functions as a retention signal, not an externalization signal. The protein catalytic domain faces the Golgi lumen. Type II Golgi glycosyltransferases are canonically endomembrane-resident and do not traffic to the PM under baseline conditions. No documented PM minority pool, PM cycling, cell-state-induced surface display, cancer-cell ecto-presentation, cell-death-related externalization, or stable surface-partner tethering. Not a secreted protein. Not a pMHC-only intracellular target.
